Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated creatinine, indicating impaired renal function. Urinalysis reveals significant proteinuria, ketonuria, glucosuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal), suggesting possible nephrotic syndrome or diabetic nephropathy, though HbA1c is low. No evidence of urinary infection. Recommend further nephrological evaluation and assessment for underlying metabolic or renal pathology.
